questions.stem: The books ______ to the library every Friday afternoon.
questions.explanation: 本题考查一般现在时的被动语态。主语The books与动词return之间是被动关系（书被归还），且时间状语every Friday afternoon表示习惯性动作，应用一般现在时的被动语态：am/is/are + 过去分词。主语是复数，故用are returned。A是主动语态；C是过去式，主动语态；D是过去时的被动语态，时态不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem:
—Where is Lucy? I haven't seen her for days.
—She ______ to Beijing. She ______ there three days ago.
questions.explanation: 本题考查现在完成时与一般过去时的区别。第一空：has gone to表示'去了某地（现在不在说话现场）'，has been to表示'去过某地（现在已经回来）'。根据语境，Lucy现在不在这里，应用has gone。第二空：three days ago是明确的过去时间状语，只能用一般过去时went。口诀：'ago用过去，完成看影响'。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The student ______ won the English speech contest is from Class 8.
questions.explanation: 本题考查定语从句关系代词的选择。先行词The student指人，且在从句中作主语，应用who或that。which指物；whom指人但只能作宾语；whose表示'……的'，作定语。技巧：指人主格用who，宾格用whom，物用which，人和物都可用的that要记熟。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: The science project ______ finished by next Friday, or we will miss the deadline.
questions.explanation: 本题考查含有情态动词的被动语态。结构为：情态动词 + be + 过去分词。主语The science project与finish是被动关系，且有情态动词must，故用must be finished。A和C缺少be动词，不能构成被动；D.need作为情态动词时通常用于否定句和疑问句，且语义不符。
questions.label: 语法题

questions.stem: If you add the prefix 'un-' to 'happy', you get 'unhappy', meaning 'not happy'. Similarly, if you add 'dis-' to 'agree', you get 'disagree', which means ______.
questions.explanation: 本题考查否定前缀。un-和dis-都是常见的否定前缀，表示'不、非'。disagree = dis- (not) + agree，意为'不同意'。常见前缀：un- (unable), dis- (dislike), re- (reuse), im- (impossible)。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: When parents are not at home, children should learn to ______ themselves instead of depending on others.
questions.explanation: 本题考查短语动词辨析。look after意为'照顾、照料'，符合句意'学会照顾自己'。look for寻找；look up查阅（词典）、向上看；look at看。技巧：after有'跟随、在……之后'的意思，引申为'跟在身后照顾'。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.stem: The weather in Shenzhen is ______ hot in July. The temperature often reaches 35°C or higher.
questions.explanation: 本题考查词义辨析。quite是副词，意为'相当、很'，修饰形容词hot。quietly安静地（副词）；quick快速的（形容词）；quiet安静的（形容词）。技巧：quite和quiet只有一个字母之差，但quite中'e'在前，可联想为'程度相当'；quiet中'et'在后，可联想为'安静的结束'。
questions.label: 词汇题

questions.group_passage:
Last term, our science teacher Mr. Wang asked us to work on a project about renewable energy. My classmates and I decided to (8)______ a model of a wind turbine.

At first, we thought it would be easy. We collected some materials from home, such as plastic bottles and cardboard. However, when we started (9)______, we faced many problems. The blades of our turbine wouldn't spin properly no matter how hard we tried.

Just when we wanted to give up, Mr. Wang came to help us. He (10)______ that the angle of the blades was wrong. After adjusting them carefully, our turbine finally worked! We were so excited that we cheered loudly.

This project taught us an important lesson: failure is not the end. (11)______, it is a chance to learn and improve. We also realized that teamwork is essential when facing (12)______. Without everyone's effort, we couldn't have finished the project successfully.

Now, our model is displayed in the school science hall.
questions.explanation: 根据上下文，老师要求做关于可再生能源的项目，后面提到收集材料（plastic bottles and cardboard），应该是'制作'一个风力涡轮机模型。make a model是固定搭配。buy（买）与收集材料制作不符；draw（画）和find（找）与后文的制作细节不符。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last term, our science teacher Mr. Wang asked us to work on a project about renewable energy. My classmates and I decided to (8)______ a model of a wind turbine.

At first, we thought it would be easy. We collected some materials from home, such as plastic bottles and cardboard. However, when we started (9)______, we faced many problems. The blades of our turbine wouldn't spin properly no matter how hard we tried.

Just when we wanted to give up, Mr. Wang came to help us. He (10)______ that the angle of the blades was wrong. After adjusting them carefully, our turbine finally worked! We were so excited that we cheered loudly.

This project taught us an important lesson: failure is not the end. (11)______, it is a chance to learn and improve. We also realized that teamwork is essential when facing (12)______. Without everyone's effort, we couldn't have finished the project successfully.

Now, our model is displayed in the school science hall.
questions.explanation: 前文提到收集材料制作模型，此处应该是开始'建造/制作'。start doing sth.，building表示建造，符合语境。playing（玩）、resting（休息）与制作模型的严肃过程不符；discussing（讨论）虽然可能发生在制作前，但后文说'遇到问题'，更可能是实际动手制作时遇到的问题。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last term, our science teacher Mr. Wang asked us to work on a project about renewable energy. My classmates and I decided to (8)______ a model of a wind turbine.

At first, we thought it would be easy. We collected some materials from home, such as plastic bottles and cardboard. However, when we started (9)______, we faced many problems. The blades of our turbine wouldn't spin properly no matter how hard we tried.

Just when we wanted to give up, Mr. Wang came to help us. He (10)______ that the angle of the blades was wrong. After adjusting them carefully, our turbine finally worked! We were so excited that we cheered loudly.

This project taught us an important lesson: failure is not the end. (11)______, it is a chance to learn and improve. We also realized that teamwork is essential when facing (12)______. Without everyone's effort, we couldn't have finished the project successfully.

Now, our model is displayed in the school science hall.
questions.explanation: 老师来帮忙，应该是'解释'问题所在（叶片角度错了）。explained（解释）符合语境。guessed（猜测）不够确定；promised（承诺）与指出问题无关；dreamed（梦想）不符合。此处体现老师指导学生解决问题的过程。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last term, our science teacher Mr. Wang asked us to work on a project about renewable energy. My classmates and I decided to (8)______ a model of a wind turbine.

At first, we thought it would be easy. We collected some materials from home, such as plastic bottles and cardboard. However, when we started (9)______, we faced many problems. The blades of our turbine wouldn't spin properly no matter how hard we tried.

Just when we wanted to give up, Mr. Wang came to help us. He (10)______ that the angle of the blades was wrong. After adjusting them carefully, our turbine finally worked! We were so excited that we cheered loudly.

This project taught us an important lesson: failure is not the end. (11)______, it is a chance to learn and improve. We also realized that teamwork is essential when facing (12)______. Without everyone's effort, we couldn't have finished the project successfully.

Now, our model is displayed in the school science hall.
questions.explanation: 考查逻辑关系。前句说'失败不是终点'，后句说'它是学习和提高的机会'，前后是转折替代关系，用Instead（相反、反而）。However虽然也表示转折，但通常引出对比内容；Instead强调'不是前者，而是后者'，更符合'失败不是结束，而是机会'的对比关系。Therefore因此；Besides此外。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Last term, our science teacher Mr. Wang asked us to work on a project about renewable energy. My classmates and I decided to (8)______ a model of a wind turbine.

At first, we thought it would be easy. We collected some materials from home, such as plastic bottles and cardboard. However, when we started (9)______, we faced many problems. The blades of our turbine wouldn't spin properly no matter how hard we tried.

Just when we wanted to give up, Mr. Wang came to help us. He (10)______ that the angle of the blades was wrong. After adjusting them carefully, our turbine finally worked! We were so excited that we cheered loudly.

This project taught us an important lesson: failure is not the end. (11)______, it is a chance to learn and improve. We also realized that teamwork is essential when facing (12)______. Without everyone's effort, we couldn't have finished the project successfully.

Now, our model is displayed in the school science hall.
questions.explanation: 考查名词词义辨析。face challenges是固定搭配，意为'面对挑战'。前文提到项目制作中遇到很多困难（problems），这些都是挑战。choices选择；changes变化；chances机会。制作模型的困难应称为challenges（挑战），团队合作用来应对挑战。
questions.label: 完形填空

questions.group_passage:
Smart Habits for Digital Life

In today's world, digital devices have become an important part of our daily life. However, using them wisely is crucial for our health and study. Here are some tips from Dr. Chen, a scientist who studies technology's impact on teenagers.

First, take regular breaks. Dr. Chen suggests following the "20-20-20" rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds. This helps reduce eye strain (疲劳) caused by staring at screens for too long.

Second, set a "digital curfew" (宵禁). Many students like to check their phones before sleeping, but the blue light from screens can make it harder to fall asleep. Dr. Chen recommends putting devices away at least one hour before bedtime. Instead, try reading a paper book or doing meditation (冥想).

Third, use technology for learning. There are many educational apps that can help us study more efficiently. However, we should be careful not to get distracted by games or social media when using these tools.

"Technology is neither good nor bad," says Dr. Chen. "It depends on how we use it. If we develop healthy digital habits now, we will benefit from technology throughout our lives."
questions.stem: What is the "20-20-20" rule mentioned in Paragraph 2?
options: A. A study method for exams
B. A rule to protect eyes
C. A way to improve sleep
D. A tool for online learning
questions.explanation: 细节理解题。根据第二段最后一句'This helps reduce eye strain (疲劳) caused by staring at screens for too long'（这有助于减少长时间盯着屏幕造成的眼疲劳），可知这是保护眼睛的规则。A是学习方法；C是改善睡眠（对应第三段）；D是在线学习工具。定位原文关键句是解题关键。
questions.label: 阅读-细节理解

questions.group_passage:
Smart Habits for Digital Life

In today's world, digital devices have become an important part of our daily life. However, using them wisely is crucial for our health and study. Here are some tips from Dr. Chen, a scientist who studies technology's impact on teenagers.

First, take regular breaks. Dr. Chen suggests following the "20-20-20" rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds. This helps reduce eye strain (疲劳) caused by staring at screens for too long.

Second, set a "digital curfew" (宵禁). Many students like to check their phones before sleeping, but the blue light from screens can make it harder to fall asleep. Dr. Chen recommends putting devices away at least one hour before bedtime. Instead, try reading a paper book or doing meditation (冥想).

Third, use technology for learning. There are many educational apps that can help us study more efficiently. However, we should be careful not to get distracted by games or social media when using these tools.

"Technology is neither good nor bad," says Dr. Chen. "It depends on how we use it. If we develop healthy digital habits now, we will benefit from technology throughout our lives."
questions.stem: What can we infer from Dr. Chen's words in the last paragraph?
options: A. Technology will replace traditional learning methods soon.
B. Students should avoid using digital devices completely.
C. The effect of technology depends on how users use it.
D. Only scientists know the correct way to use technology.
questions.explanation: 推理判断题。根据最后一段Dr. Chen的话'Technology is neither good nor bad... It depends on how we use it'（科技本身不好也不坏，取决于我们如何使用），可推断出C选项'科技的影响取决于使用者如何使用'。A选项'取代传统学习'文中未提；B选项'完全避免使用'与文章鼓励健康使用而非禁止使用的观点相反；D选项'只有科学家知道正确方法'过于绝对，且与原文'我们都能培养健康习惯'不符。
questions.label: 阅读-推理判断

questions.group_passage:
Smart Habits for Digital Life

In today's world, digital devices have become an important part of our daily life. However, using them wisely is crucial for our health and study. Here are some tips from Dr. Chen, a scientist who studies technology's impact on teenagers.

First, take regular breaks. Dr. Chen suggests following the "20-20-20" rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds. This helps reduce eye strain (疲劳) caused by staring at screens for too long.

Second, set a "digital curfew" (宵禁). Many students like to check their phones before sleeping, but the blue light from screens can make it harder to fall asleep. Dr. Chen recommends putting devices away at least one hour before bedtime. Instead, try reading a paper book or doing meditation (冥想).

Third, use technology for learning. There are many educational apps that can help us study more efficiently. However, we should be careful not to get distracted by games or social media when using these tools.

"Technology is neither good nor bad," says Dr. Chen. "It depends on how we use it. If we develop healthy digital habits now, we will benefit from technology throughout our lives."
questions.stem: What does the word "distracted" in Paragraph 4 probably mean?
questions.explanation: 词义猜测题。根据上下文'use technology for learning'（用科技学习）和'by games or social media'（被游戏或社交媒体），可推断distracted意为'使分心'。学习时如果不小心，就会被游戏和社交媒体分散注意力。技巧：根据上下文逻辑，学习工具与游戏/社交媒体是竞争关系，后者会干扰前者，故为'分心'。
questions.label: 阅读-词义猜测

questions.group_passage:
Smart Habits for Digital Life

In today's world, digital devices have become an important part of our daily life. However, using them wisely is crucial for our health and study. Here are some tips from Dr. Chen, a scientist who studies technology's impact on teenagers.

First, take regular breaks. Dr. Chen suggests following the "20-20-20" rule: every 20 minutes, look at something 20 feet away for at least 20 seconds. This helps reduce eye strain (疲劳) caused by staring at screens for too long.

Second, set a "digital curfew" (宵禁). Many students like to check their phones before sleeping, but the blue light from screens can make it harder to fall asleep. Dr. Chen recommends putting devices away at least one hour before bedtime. Instead, try reading a paper book or doing meditation (冥想).

Third, use technology for learning. There are many educational apps that can help us study more efficiently. However, we should be careful not to get distracted by games or social media when using these tools.

"Technology is neither good nor bad," says Dr. Chen. "It depends on how we use it. If we develop healthy digital habits now, we will benefit from technology throughout our lives."
questions.stem: What is the best title for this passage?
options: A. The History of Digital Devices
B. Smart Habits for Digital Life
C. The Dangers of Using Technology
D. How to Make Digital Devices
questions.explanation: 主旨大意题。文章开头即点明主旨'using them wisely is crucial'，随后分三点介绍健康使用数字设备的建议，最后总结要培养健康习惯。B选项'数字生活的聪明习惯'准确概括全文。A选项'数字设备的历史'未提及；C选项'使用科技的危险'过于负面，文章是建议而非恐吓；D选项'如何制作数字设备'与内容无关。技巧：找首尾段主题句，关注重复出现的关键词（habits, wisely, healthy）。
questions.label: 阅读-主旨大意

questions.group_passage:
Having a good daily routine is important for middle school students. It helps us manage time better and reduces stress. Here are some suggestions for improving your school life.

(17)______ Try to go to bed before 10:30 p.m. and wake up at 6:30 a.m. This ensures you get 8 hours of sleep, which is necessary for teenagers.

(18)______ Don't just memorize facts. Try to understand the main ideas and connect new knowledge with what you already know. Taking notes in class is also helpful.

(19)______ Physical exercise not only keeps you healthy but also improves your concentration. You can play basketball, run, or simply take a walk after school.

Finally, don't forget to communicate with your parents and friends. (20)______ Sharing your feelings can help you deal with pressure and feel more supported.
options: A. First, keep a regular sleep schedule.
B. Then, find a quiet place to study.
C. Second, develop effective study habits.
E. In this way, you can build stronger relationships.
questions.explanation: 考查段落衔接。空后内容讲的是晚上10:30前睡觉、早上6:30起床、保证8小时睡眠，这些都是关于睡眠规律的。A选项'首先，保持规律的睡眠时间表'与之匹配。B选项提到学习地点，与该段睡眠主题不符；C是学习习惯；D是锻炼；E是建立关系。注意段落结构词First, Second等的使用。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Having a good daily routine is important for middle school students. It helps us manage time better and reduces stress. Here are some suggestions for improving your school life.

(17)______ Try to go to bed before 10:30 p.m. and wake up at 6:30 a.m. This ensures you get 8 hours of sleep, which is necessary for teenagers.

(18)______ Don't just memorize facts. Try to understand the main ideas and connect new knowledge with what you already know. Taking notes in class is also helpful.

(19)______ Physical exercise not only keeps you healthy but also improves your concentration. You can play basketball, run, or simply take a walk after school.

Finally, don't forget to communicate with your parents and friends. (20)______ Sharing your feelings can help you deal with pressure and feel more supported.
options: A. First, keep a regular sleep schedule.
B. Then, find a quiet place to study.
C. Second, develop effective study habits.
E. In this way, you can build stronger relationships.
questions.explanation: 考查段落衔接。空后内容讲的是不要死记硬背、要理解主旨、联系旧知识、记笔记，这些都是学习方法。C选项'第二，培养有效的学习习惯'与之匹配。注意段落结构词：前文有First，此处用Second形成顺序。A已被选用；B未在该段体现；D是运动；E是人际关系。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Having a good daily routine is important for middle school students. It helps us manage time better and reduces stress. Here are some suggestions for improving your school life.

(17)______ Try to go to bed before 10:30 p.m. and wake up at 6:30 a.m. This ensures you get 8 hours of sleep, which is necessary for teenagers.

(18)______ Don't just memorize facts. Try to understand the main ideas and connect new knowledge with what you already know. Taking notes in class is also helpful.

(19)______ Physical exercise not only keeps you healthy but also improves your concentration. You can play basketball, run, or simply take a walk after school.

Finally, don't forget to communicate with your parents and friends. (20)______ Sharing your feelings can help you deal with pressure and feel more supported.
options: A. First, keep a regular sleep schedule.
B. Then, find a quiet place to study.
C. Second, develop effective study habits.
E. In this way, you can build stronger relationships.
questions.explanation: 考查段落衔接。空后内容讲的是体育锻炼不仅保持健康还能提高专注力，可以打篮球、跑步或散步。D选项'此外，定期锻炼'与之匹配。Besides表示补充关系，用于列举另一条建议。A、C已用；B未体现；E是人际关系。
questions.label: 短文填空

questions.group_passage:
Having a good daily routine is important for middle school students. It helps us manage time better and reduces stress. Here are some suggestions for improving your school life.

(17)______ Try to go to bed before 10:30 p.m. and wake up at 6:30 a.m. This ensures you get 8 hours of sleep, which is necessary for teenagers.

(18)______ Don't just memorize facts. Try to understand the main ideas and connect new knowledge with what you already know. Taking notes in class is also helpful.

(19)______ Physical exercise not only keeps you healthy but also improves your concentration. You can play basketball, run, or simply take a walk after school.

Finally, don't forget to communicate with your parents and friends. (20)______ Sharing your feelings can help you deal with pressure and feel more supported.
options: A. First, keep a regular sleep schedule.
B. Then, find a quiet place to study.
C. Second, develop effective study habits.
E. In this way, you can build stronger relationships.
questions.explanation: 考查段落衔接。空前讲与父母朋友交流，空后讲分享感受有助于应对压力。E选项'这样，你可以建立更牢固的关系'承上启下，说明交流沟通的结果。In this way指代前文的'communicate with your parents and friends'。A、C、D已用；B是干扰项（安静的学习地点未在文中提及）。
questions.label: 短文填空
